moat.description: Dominant scale, switching costs (Prime), and cost advantage (Logistics). AWS backlog hits $244B+, up 40%+ YoY. Amazon has now committed $33B total to Anthropic ($8B prior + $25B new deal at $380B valuation, announced April 20, 2026) — and Anthropic in turn committed $100B+ in AWS spend over the next 10 years, with 5 GW of capacity additions and access to Trainium2 through Trainium4. This bilateral lock-in (Amazon funds Anthropic; Anthropic is Anthropic's primary cloud) is structurally unmatched by any competitor. Anthropic Mythos and Project Glasswing run on AWS Bedrock. Competitive pressure from Azure (+39% YoY) and GCP (+36% YoY) is a monitored risk but the Anthropic $100B commitment provides a 10-year AWS demand floor.
moat.analysisSummary: Amazon possesses a **Wide Economic Moat** driven by four primary pillars:

analysisPoints.title: Cost Advantage
analysisPoints.text: Its massive fulfillment infrastructure creates unit costs that no competitor can match, allowing for faster delivery and lower prices.

analysisPoints.title: Switching Costs
analysisPoints.text: The Prime ecosystem locks in consumers. Once a household is integrated into Prime, the convenience makes shopping elsewhere a "costly" friction.

analysisPoints.title: Network Effect
analysisPoints.text: The 3rd party marketplace creates a flywheel where more sellers attract more buyers, which attracts more sellers.

analysisPoints.title: AWS as the Primary AI Training Cloud
analysisPoints.text: Amazon has now invested $33B total in Anthropic ($8B prior + $25B new, April 20, 2026) — locking in AWS as the exclusive primary cloud for training and deploying Claude models at a $380B Anthropic valuation. Crucially, Anthropic committed to spending $100B+ on AWS technologies over the next 10 years, adding 5 GW of capacity and using Trainium2 through Trainium4 (including future chip generations). This is a bilateral strategic lock-in: Amazon funds Anthropic's growth; Anthropic is structurally dependent on AWS for every Claude API call, Bedrock workload, and frontier model training run. Anthropic's $30B+ annualised revenue (up from $9B at end-2025) is entirely routed through AWS infrastructure — making Anthropic the single fastest-growing AWS revenue driver. The $100B commitment provides a 10-year structural demand floor for AWS that no competing cloud can replicate.

growth.description: AWS re-accelerated to 28% in Q1 2026 — fastest growth in 15 quarters — beating the 24% pace of Q4 2025 and landing ahead of the 25–26% consensus. Q1 revenue came in at $181.5B (+16% YoY), beating the $177.3B consensus by $4.2B. Operating income of $23.9B crushed the $16.5–21.5B guidance range. Amazon's chips business (Graviton, Trainium, Nitro) has crossed $20B annual revenue run rate growing triple digits YoY — an emerging second AWS moat. Q2 guidance of $194–199B implies continued ~15–17% YoY revenue growth. Tariff headwinds (125–145% on Chinese goods) remain, but the operational beat and AWS trajectory confirm the re-acceleration thesis.

additionalNote.title: Financial Outlook
points: AWS re-accelerated to 28% YoY in Q1 2026 ($37.6B revenue) — fastest in 15 quarters, +4pp vs Q4 2025 (24%) — confirming the re-acceleration thesis. Q2 guide $194–199B implies continued double-digit revenue growth.

Amazon chips (Graviton, Trainium3, Nitro) crossed $20B annual revenue run rate growing triple digits YoY; Trainium2 is fully subscribed, Trainium3 launched at 40% better price-performance, Project Rainier deploying 500K+ Trainium2 chips for Anthropic — silicon becoming a durable AWS moat.
Amazon committed $33B total to Anthropic ($8B prior + $25B new, April 2026); Anthropic committed $100B+ in AWS spend over 10 years — the largest bilateral AI infrastructure lock-in in cloud history.

Tariff headwinds persist (125–145% on Chinese goods); Q1 operating income of $23.9B crushed the $16.5–21.5B guidance range, demonstrating that tariff impacts are being managed. Advertising and logistics efficiency are offsetting marketplace pressure.

growthAnalysis.scoreDerivation: Base 80 (15–30% blended CAGR) + 5 recurring revenue (AWS contracts + Prime) + 5 TAM expansion (AI inference + sovereign cloud + chips) − 2 Azure/GCP competitive acceleration (40%/63% vs AWS 28%) + 1 Anthropic/Bedrock demand confirmed − 1 tariff/marketplace headwinds (Chinese sellers) = 88

drivers.name: AWS Cloud
drivers.metric: +28% YoY (Q1 2026), $37.6B — fastest in 15 quarters; chips >$20B ARR triple-digit YoY

drivers.name: Advertising
drivers.metric: +23% YoY, $21.3B Q4 ($85B+ annualised path); Q1 2026 data pending confirmation

drivers.name: International Retail
drivers.metric: +16% Q1 2026 total revenue; margins expanding as capex cycle matures

growthAnalysis.keyRisk: Azure (+40% Q3 FY26) and GCP (+63% Q1 2026) are growing materially faster than AWS (28%); if this gap widens and they capture the majority of new enterprise AI inference workloads, AWS decelerates below 20%, the $200B 2026 capex cycle proves premature, and FCF compression extends to 2028+

valuation.description: Q1 2026 delivered a decisive confirmation: AWS at 28% (fastest in 15 quarters), operating income $23.9B (blew out the $16.5–21.5B guidance range), and chips crossing $20B ARR growing triple digits. We raise the base fair value from $270 to $295 to reflect the Q2 guide of $194–199B and the emerging chips business. At $264, the stock sits 10% below the new base case — offering a modest margin of safety post-confirmation. The bull case rises to $365 reflecting the silicon moat thesis playing out.

valuationNote.text: Using a 10-year DCF with a 9% WACC and a 3.5% terminal growth rate, incorporating Q1 2026 actuals (AWS 28%, operating income $23.9B) and Q2 guide ($194–199B), our fair value estimate for AMZN is

rows.label: Forward P/E (NTM)
rows.note: NTM EPS ~$9.00 (revised up on Q1 beat)

peAnalysis.summary: At ~29× forward earnings post-Q1 beat, AMZN has de-rated from the prior ~32× as the earnings ramp materialises faster than consensus expected. AWS at 28% (Q1 2026) confirms the re-acceleration thesis. The PEG of 1.61× reflects a more attractive entry point than the prior 1.80× as earnings upgrade momentum builds. The $200B capex cycle is beginning to monetise: chips at $20B+ ARR growing triple digits is a new revenue line that was not in any prior model.

bear.description: Persistent 125–145% tariffs cause marketplace GMV disruption; AWS growth decelerates as Azure and GCP win disproportionate enterprise AI inference workloads despite the Q1 2026 re-acceleration, and FCF compression extends to 2028+.
points: Chinese sellers (~50%+ of marketplace GMV) reduce listings or raise prices materially post-tariff escalation, eroding Amazon's low-price advantage and consumer traffic; FBA rate increases accelerate seller churn

AWS decelerates back below 20% as Azure (+40% Q3 FY26) and Google Cloud (+63% Q1 2026) continue winning enterprise AI inference — the Q1 2026 28% print proves to be a one-quarter spike, not a durable re-acceleration
$200B capex drives FCF near-zero for 2026–2027; chips business ARR growth slows; multiple compresses to 22–24× forward earnings

base.description: AWS sustains 25–28% growth through 2026, chips business scales past $30B ARR, advertising reaches $90B+ annualised, and FCF rebounds above $50B by 2027 as the capex cycle matures.
points: AWS sustains 25–28% growth with Q2 2026 guide ($194–199B total) on track; the Trainium/Graviton/Nitro chips business scales from $20B to $30B+ ARR as Trainium3 ramps (40% better price-performance than T2)
Advertising sustains 20%+ growth reaching $90B+ annualised run rate; Anthropic's $30B+ revenue routing through AWS Bedrock compounds demand visibility
FCF rebounds above $50B by 2027 as the $200B capex cycle matures; tariff headwinds absorbed by operational leverage and marketplace mix shifts

bull.description: AWS re-accelerates above 30%, chips business at $40B+ ARR becomes Amazon's third trillion-dollar segment, and the capex cycle delivers transformational FCF by 2027–2028.
points: AWS sustains 30%+ growth as Anthropic's $100B AWS commitment (5 GW capacity, Trainium2–4) and sovereign AI demand drive the backlog above $350B; Bedrock becomes the default enterprise AI inference platform

Chips (Graviton + Trainium + Nitro) scale past $40B ARR growing triple digits — Trainium3 achieves cost-performance parity with Nvidia H100/B200 creating a 2–3 year silicon moat that cements AWS as the lowest-cost AI training and inference cloud
FCF surpasses $80B by 2027–2028 as capex transitions to depreciation tailwinds; buybacks announced, triggering re-rating toward 35× forward earnings

learnedInterfaces.note: Alexa voice interface and AWS console are table stakes easily replicated; not a durable differentiator.

businessLogic.note: AI is commoditizing logistics routing and retail recommendation logic that once required years to build.

publicDataAccess.note: Amazon's 200M+ product reviews and buyer behaviour signals still provide meaningful aggregation advantages, but AI scraping and competitor datasets have eroded the edge. The moat persists in scale, not exclusivity.

talentScarcity.note: AWS cloud operations require fewer skilled human operators thanks to AI-powered automation.

bundling.note: Prime bundle (shipping + video + music + Alexa + Pharmacy + Gaming) is deeply differentiated and drives 200M+ loyalty.

proprietaryData.note: Purchase intent data, AWS usage telemetry, and last-mile logistics operational data are genuinely irreplaceable. AWS $244B+ backlog reflects deep customer commitment. The Anthropic bilateral lock-in ($33B Amazon investment; $100B+ Anthropic AWS commitment over 10 years) creates a proprietary data feedback loop: Anthropic's training workloads on Trainium2–4 generate silicon telemetry no competitor can observe, compounding AWS's custom chip advantage. This is now a structural, not just financial, relationship.

regulatoryLockIn.note: AWS GovCloud, DoD JEDI, HIPAA, FedRAMP, and financial services compliance create enormous switching friction.

networkEffects.note: Marketplace two-sided flywheel: more buyers → more sellers → better selection → more buyers. Self-reinforcing.

transactionEmbedding.note: 1-click purchasing habits, Prime subscription, and AWS embedded in the infrastructure of the global internet.

systemOfRecord.note: AWS is the system of record for global cloud infrastructure; $142B annualized run rate and S3 stores more data than any competitor by a wide margin.

tenMoats.verdict: Amazon's moats are overwhelmingly AI-resilient: the $244B AWS backlog, Trainium custom silicon, proprietary purchase data, and the marketplace flywheel are core strengths that are strengthening — not weakening — in the AI era.
